plain: The maximum the insurer will pay for a covered service.
The portion of the billed charge the provider has agreed to forgive under their contract.
A fixed dollar amount you pay at the time of service.
A percentage you pay after meeting your deductible.
The amount you pay out of pocket before the insurer starts paying.
The most you have to pay in a year before the insurer pays 100% of covered services.
Coordination of benefits: the rules that decide which insurance pays first when you have more than one.
When a provider bills you for the difference between their charge and what insurance allowed. Restricted by the No Surprises Act in many cases.
Provider has a contract with your plan; lower allowed amounts apply.
Provider has no contract with your plan; higher cost-sharing typically applies.
The window in which a claim must be submitted, set by the payer.
A statutory or contractual deadline for the payer to pay a clean claim.
